name: Deliver a metrics report with Airtable and Telegram in real time

parameters.text: =✅ Deliver a metrics report with Airtable and Telegram in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Recent from Airtable

### Read Recent from Airtable

Searches Airtable's 'Events' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Ports: ①→ Render as HTML · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Render as HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Post Digest to Telegram

### Post Digest to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Post Digest to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Confirm All Sends

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Archive Digest in Airtable

### Archive Digest in Airtable

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Events' table.

Ports: ①→ Load Previous Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Event Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per event (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Events' table.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Telegram · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Deliver a metrics report with Airtable and Telegram in real time

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Deliver a metrics report with Airtable and Telegram in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual thresholds is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Recent from Airtable → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Render as HTML → Post Digest to Telegram → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating thresholds in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Responds in seconds
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2 / service account; OpenRouter API key; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Summarization uses a Chain, not an Agent, because the step is fixed — there's no decision about which tool to call, just 'summarize this text.' Posting to multiple channels runs as parallel branches, not sequential sends, so a slow API on one channel never delays the message that was already ready on another.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Add a third notification channel and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Change 'Trim to Digest Size' from 15 to 5 and see how it narrows the summary's scope.
3. Swap the Summarization Chain for a Q&A Chain to answer questions instead of just recapping.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your thresholds process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
